title: Home glucose log review

red_flags.text: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.
red_flags.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-recorded concern features in an established diabetes review and cannot classify an entered reading or urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

examination.text: Supports clinician-recorded initial, follow-up and annual medical history, medicines, hypoglycaemia, monitoring, blood pressure, weight, examination, laboratory and complication-screening context.
examination.rationale: Relevant to established-diabetes follow-up domains and already available monitoring or laboratory context, not diagnosis or treatment.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-recorded initial, follow-up and annual medical history, medicines, hypoglycaemia, monitoring, blood pressure, weight, examination, laboratory and complication-screening context.

source.title: 4. Comprehensive Medical Evaluation and Assessment of Comorbidities: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

exact_section.heading: Components of the comprehensive diabetes medical evaluation

investigations.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
investigations.rationale: Relevant to established type 2 diabetes follow-up context, symptoms, complications and clinician review; it does not validate a generic glucose log or infer control.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

investigations.text: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.
investigations.rationale: Relevant to recording already available laboratory context only; it does not generate testing or results.
investigations.evidence_extract: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

exact_section.heading: Appendix 7 — summary of required laboratory tests

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Relevant to clinician review of entered blood-glucose records, method and accuracy concerns without interpreting readings.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting monitoring method, circumstances, treatment context, suspected inaccuracy, interfering substances and reviewed blood-glucose records.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Relevant to documenting CGM use, reviewed data and device concerns without calculating or interpreting CGM metrics.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting CGM use, individual preference, continuity, periodic use, skin reaction, interfering-substance and reviewed-data context without changing therapy.

source.title: 7. Diabetes Technology: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

medication.text: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.
medication.rationale: Relevant to clinician-led monitoring documentation but does not prescribe frequency or interpret values in this scaffold.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30

follow_up.text: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.
follow_up.rationale: Relevant to recording monitoring method, preferences, training and data-sharing context without selecting a device.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting individual circumstances, preferences, caregiver role, device training, data sharing and clinician support needs.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
No readings, dates, meal context, symptoms, device, diabetes type, treatment, pregnancy, age, ketones, laboratory values or clinician interpretation are present.
No automatic diagnosis, calculation, target, urgency, monitoring frequency, medicine change, dietary advice or referral is supported.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
